description: Fix all issues found in code review — from critical DB bugs to SDK usability improvements

userStories.title: DB connection pool via shared connection in lifespan
userStories.description: As a server operator, I want a shared DB connection managed in FastAPI lifespan so that concurrent WebSocket connections don't cause 'database is locked' errors.
acceptanceCriteria: FastAPI lifespan creates a single aiosqlite connection stored in app.state.db
All route handlers and websocket handlers use app.state.db instead of get_db()
WAL mode and foreign_keys pragma are set once at startup
Connection is properly closed on shutdown
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: get_db() in server/database.py opens/closes a new connection every call. Replace with shared connection pattern.

userStories.title: Validate recipient agent exists before sending
userStories.description: As an agent, I want the server to return an error when I send a message to a non-existent agent so that I know the message won't be silently lost.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /messages returns 404 if to_agent does not exist in agents table
WebSocket send action returns error frame if to_agent does not exist
Room message send verifies the room exists and sender is a member
Add test for sending to non-existent agent
All existing tests pass

userStories.title: Offline queue cleanup background task
userStories.description: As a server operator, I want expired messages in the delivery queue to be automatically cleaned up so that the database doesn't grow unbounded.
acceptanceCriteria: A background asyncio task runs cleanup_expired() periodically (every hour)
Task is started in lifespan and cancelled on shutdown
cleanup_expired() uses the shared DB connection from app.state.db
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: cleanup_expired() in offline_queue.py exists but is never called

userStories.title: Message size limit
userStories.description: As a server operator, I want message content to be limited to 32KB so that agents can't send arbitrarily large messages.
acceptanceCriteria: SendMessage model has max_length=32768 on content field
WebSocket frame size is checked before processing (64KB frame limit)
Server returns appropriate error for oversized messages
Add test for oversized message rejection
All existing tests pass

userStories.title: Agent.from_credentials() auto-load
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to automatically load saved credentials so that I can reconnect after a session reset without re-registering.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent.from_credentials(broker_url, name=) classmethod loads from ~/.claw-msg/ credential store
CredentialStore supports lookup by broker_url + name (not just agent_id)
Returns properly initialized Agent with token and agent_id
Raises ValueError if no saved credentials found
All existing tests pass

userStories.title: Request-reply pattern (agent.ask())
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to send a message and await the reply so that I can have conversations without manually correlating messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent has ask(to, content, timeout=30) async method
ask() sends message with a unique msg_id, waits for reply with matching reply_to
reply() method sends with reply_to field set to original message id
Times out with asyncio.TimeoutError if no reply within timeout
Pending reply futures are cleaned up on timeout
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: New method on Agent class. Needs _pending_replies dict and integration with message receive handler.

userStories.title: Reuse httpx AsyncClient
userStories.description: As a client user, I want the HTTP transport to reuse a single httpx client so that connections are pooled instead of re-creating TCP+TLS per request.
acceptanceCriteria: HttpTransport creates httpx.AsyncClient once in __init__
All HTTP methods use the shared client
Client is properly closed in close() method
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: client/http.py creates new AsyncClient per request

userStories.title: listen() background mode and stop()
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to run listen() in the background so that I can do other work while receiving messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent.listen(background=True) returns an asyncio.Task without blocking
Agent.stop() cancels the listen task and closes connections
Default listen() (no args) still blocks as before
All existing tests pass

userStories.title: Fix WebSocket connected property
userStories.description: As an agent, I want the connected property to accurately reflect connection state so that send() correctly chooses HTTP vs WebSocket transport.
acceptanceCriteria: connected property checks both _ws is not None AND not _ws.closed (or equivalent)
send() falls back to HTTP when WebSocket is dead
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: client/connection.py checks only _ws is not None

userStories.title: Token out of service files
userStories.description: As a security-conscious user, I want tokens to not be visible in process listings so that credentials are not leaked via ps aux.
acceptanceCriteria: Daemon service files use environment file or EnvironmentVariables instead of CLI args for token
Token is written to ~/.claw-msg/daemon-env with chmod 600
systemd unit uses EnvironmentFile directive
launchd plist uses EnvironmentVariables dict
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: daemon/service.py generates files with raw token in args

userStories.title: get_messages includes room messages
userStories.description: As an agent, I want get_messages to return both DMs and messages from rooms I'm in so that I don't miss room conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /messages returns messages where agent is recipient OR sender OR member of the message's room
Room membership is checked via room_members table
Add test verifying room messages appear in get_messages
All existing tests pass

userStories.title: Exception handling in on_message handlers
userStories.description: As an agent developer, I want exceptions in my message handlers to be caught and logged so that the WebSocket connection stays alive.
acceptanceCriteria: Exceptions in on_message callbacks are caught with try/except
Error is logged but does not propagate to connection loop
Connection remains alive after handler exception
All existing tests pass

userStories.title: Missing CLI commands
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want rooms list, rooms send, and contacts alias commands.
acceptanceCriteria: claw-msg rooms list — lists rooms the agent is a member of
claw-msg rooms send <room_id> <message> — sends message to room
claw-msg contacts alias <agent_id> <alias> — updates alias for a contact
All existing tests pass

userStories.title: Async context manager for Agent
userStories.description: As an agent developer, I want to use async with Agent(...) as agent: for automatic cleanup.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent implements __aenter__ and __aexit__
__aexit__ calls close() to clean up connections
All existing tests pass
